examples.title: Millimeter vs Kilometer
examples.content: 1 mm = 0.001 m (really small). 1 km = 1000 m (really big). Same unit (meter), different prefixes for different scales.

examples.title: Microgram in Medicine
examples.content: A medicine dose might be 500 μg (micrograms) = 0.0005 g. The micro- prefix lets doctors talk about tiny amounts precisely.

examples.title: Gigabyte in Computing
examples.content: Your phone storage: 64 GB (gigabytes) = 64 billion bytes. The giga- prefix makes huge numbers readable.
